body.title: CLOSED: lever_watcher: stop-word filter + 5-min self-throttle
body.result: stop-word filter + 5-min self-throttle in watch_problems.py; test fixture reset for isolation
body.description:
lever_watcher() floods the channel when NE cycles fast on empty TWM. Two fixes in watch_problems.py: (1) add _LEVER_STOP word set (recent, recently, surfaced, problem, found, possible, studies, observations and other common English words) and raise min keyword length from >3 to >4 so noise words can't accumulate confidence; (2) add a module-level 5-min self-throttle (_LEVER_WATCHER_LAST_RUN monotonic timestamp) so the function returns 0 immediately if called within 300s of its last run — regardless of how often coa.py calls it. Root cause: watch condition 'Recently surfaced problem' contains only common words; NE empty cycles fire lever_watcher every cycle; 20+ posts/sec before in-process dedup closes the window.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/watch_problems.py (lever_watcher function + module-level stop set and throttle)
**Design rules:** OOP-first not applicable (module-level function); no-sqlite (no DB changes)
**Scope boundary:** IN: _LEVER_STOP set; len>4 filter; 5-min self-throttle via monotonic timestamp. OUT: changing watch condition strings in DB; changing confidence accumulation math; changing coa.py call site
**Test plan:** After fix, restart Igor and confirm channel is quiet (no lever burst). Verify lever_watcher still fires eventually for a genuine keyword match by checking confidence accumulation in watch_problems table. No unit test (channel integration only).
